problem: Data obligations (classification tiers, encryption requirements, PII handling, audit rules) are scattered across five tools in the distributed stack — Arbiter, Pact, Baton, Sentinel, and Constrain — each maintaining its own partial copy of schema truth. This drift creates compliance gaps: a field marked PII in one tool may be treated as PUBLIC in another, encrypted_at_rest annotations don't reach mock data generators, and migration changes to sensitive columns bypass gate checks because no central authority knows the full picture. Engineers discover mismatches in production or during audits rather than at schema registration time. There is no single place to answer "what does this data look like and what are its rules?" and no mechanism to propagate annotation changes into the control plane automatically.

places.name: CLI Entry Point
places.description: Click-based CLI dispatching to init, backend, schema, migrate, mock, export, and serve subcommands

places.name: Ledger Init
places.description: Creates .ledger/ directory structure: registry/, plans/, changelog.jsonl

places.name: Backend Registry
places.description: Flat YAML store under .ledger/registry/ tracking backend id, type, and owner component. Enforces unique backend ids and exclusive component ownership

places.name: Schema Store
places.description: Stores schema YAML files verbatim under .ledger/registry/<backend_id>/. Supports multi-table files. Append-only changelog records every schema addition

places.name: Schema Validator
places.description: Validates all registered schemas: checks annotation conflict pairs (immutable+gdpr_erasable, audit_field+gdpr_erasable, soft_delete_marker+immutable), REQUIRES satisfaction rules, backend ownership exclusivity. Returns all violations, not just the first

places.name: Annotation Propagation Table
places.description: Data-driven table mapping each annotation to its propagation targets (Pact assertion type, Arbiter tier behavior, Baton masking rule, Sentinel severity). Custom annotations from ledger.yaml extend this table without code changes

places.name: Migration Parser
places.description: Regex-based SQL DDL parser extracting ADD COLUMN, DROP COLUMN, ALTER COLUMN with field names, types, and constraints from PostgreSQL/ANSI SQL migration files

places.name: Migration Planner
places.description: Computes schema diff between current registry state and parsed migration. Applies gate logic: audit_field drop → BLOCKED, immutable modify → BLOCKED, encryption removal → HUMAN_GATE, tier outside component data_access → HUMAN_GATE, PUBLIC-only to declared component → AUTO_PROCEED. Stores plan as JSON in .ledger/plans/

places.name: Migration Approval
places.description: Records human approval for HUMAN_GATE plans with required review reference and rationale. Updates plan status in .ledger/plans/

places.name: Pact Exporter
places.description: Generates component-scoped contract assertion YAML from schema annotations and propagation table

places.name: Arbiter Exporter
places.description: Generates field-level classification rules YAML with pattern, backend, tier, annotations, taint and mask flags

places.name: Baton Exporter
places.description: Generates egress node config YAML with masked_fields and canary_eligible_fields (string-typed PII/FINANCIAL fields only)

places.name: Sentinel Exporter
places.description: Generates severity mapping YAML derived from annotation propagation rules

places.name: Mock Generator
places.description: Generates N mock records for a backend table. Uses seed from config or --seed override for determinism. Faker for PII, stdlib random for everything else. Token-shaped values for encrypted_at_rest and tokenized fields. Canary mode generates fingerprinted values and optionally registers with Arbiter

places.name: HTTP API
places.description: FastAPI + uvicorn server on configurable port (default 7701). Exposes all registry, schema, migration, export, and mock operations as REST endpoints. Starts in under 3 seconds

places.name: Config Loader
places.description: Reads ledger.yaml from project root. Provides registry path, API port, integration endpoints (arbiter, pact, stigmergy), mock seed, migration parser list, and custom annotation definitions

places.name: Changelog
places.description: Append-only JSONL file at .ledger/changelog.jsonl recording every schema change, backend registration, migration plan, and approval with timestamps

affordances.name: ledger init
affordances.description: Creates .ledger/ directory with empty registry, plans dir, and changelog

affordances.name: ledger backend add
affordances.description: Registers a storage backend with --type and --owner flags. Fails if backend id already exists

affordances.name: ledger schema add
affordances.description: Accepts a schema YAML file path, validates YAML syntax, stores verbatim, appends to changelog

affordances.name: ledger schema show
affordances.description: Displays registered schema for a backend, optionally filtered to a single table

affordances.name: ledger schema validate
affordances.description: Runs full validation across all schemas: conflict pairs, REQUIRES rules, ownership exclusivity. Returns all violations

affordances.name: ledger migrate plan
affordances.description: Parses migration file, computes diff against current schema, returns MigrationGate (BLOCKED/HUMAN_GATE/AUTO_PROCEED) with full violation list

affordances.name: ledger migrate approve
affordances.description: Approves a HUMAN_GATE plan with --review reference id and rationale text

affordances.name: ledger export
affordances.description: Exports annotations in target format: --format pact|arbiter|baton|sentinel with optional --component filter

affordances.name: ledger mock
affordances.description: Generates mock records for a table with --count, --seed, and --purpose (canary) options

affordances.name: ledger serve
affordances.description: Starts FastAPI server on configured port

affordances.name: validate annotations
affordances.description: Internal: checks field annotations against CONFLICTS and REQUIRES tables

affordances.name: parse SQL DDL
affordances.description: Internal: regex-based extraction of ADD/DROP/ALTER COLUMN from SQL migration files

affordances.name: compute schema diff
affordances.description: Internal: diffs parsed migration against registered schema to identify affected fields and their annotations

affordances.name: apply gate logic
affordances.description: Internal: evaluates diff against gate rules to produce BLOCKED/HUMAN_GATE/AUTO_PROCEED with rationale

affordances.name: resolve propagation
affordances.description: Internal: looks up annotation in propagation table and returns target-specific config fragments

affordances.name: generate token-shaped value
affordances.description: Internal: produces tok_{base64url_24chars} for encrypted_at_rest and tokenized fields

affordances.name: generate canary fingerprint
affordances.description: Internal: produces ledger-canary-{tier}-{hex8} shaped to field type for canary-eligible string fields

affordances.name: register canary with Arbiter
affordances.description: Internal: POSTs canary fingerprints to Arbiter API if configured, warns and skips if not

affordances.name: acquire file lock
affordances.description: Internal: fcntl-based file locking for changelog and plans directory writes

connections.description: When --format pact
When --format arbiter
When --format baton
When --format sentinel
Every schema addition appends to changelog.jsonl
Every backend registration appends to changelog.jsonl
Reads all registered schemas for cross-schema validation
Checks annotations against CONFLICTS and REQUIRES tables
Parses the migration file into structured changes
Loads current schema to diff against migration changes
Evaluates diff to produce gate decision
Records migration plan in changelog and .ledger/plans/
Records approval decision in changelog
Reads schema and applies Pact propagation rules
Reads schema and applies Arbiter propagation rules
Reads schema and applies Baton propagation rules
Reads schema and applies Sentinel propagation rules
Loads table schema to determine field types and annotations
For encrypted_at_rest and tokenized fields
When --purpose canary for eligible string fields
POSTs canary fingerprints if Arbiter is configured
REST endpoint proxies to registry
REST endpoint proxies to schema store
REST endpoint proxies to schema query
REST endpoint proxies to validator
REST endpoint proxies to planner
REST endpoint proxies to approval
Custom annotations from ledger.yaml extend the propagation table at startup

rabbit_holes.description: SQL migration parser scope creep: regex-based DDL parsing for PostgreSQL is tractable for ADD/DROP/ALTER COLUMN in ALTER TABLE statements, but edge cases (multi-line statements, comments, quoted identifiers, conditional DDL with IF EXISTS, column renames via multiple statements) could consume the entire cycle if we try to handle them all.
rabbit_holes.mitigation: Scope v1 parser to: ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN, ALTER TABLE DROP COLUMN, ALTER TABLE ALTER COLUMN (type change, SET/DROP NOT NULL, SET/DROP DEFAULT). Ignore CREATE TABLE (that's initial schema, not migration). Ignore RENAME COLUMN (treat as DROP + ADD in the diff). Regex patterns tested against a fixed corpus of 15-20 known SQL inputs. Anything unparseable returns HUMAN_GATE with 'unparseable statement' reason rather than crashing or silently ignoring.

rabbit_holes.description: Annotation propagation table design: making propagation fully data-driven (so custom annotations work without code changes) requires the export format generators to be generic enough to handle arbitrary propagation targets, but each export format (Pact, Arbiter, Baton, Sentinel) has a different output shape. There's a tension between 'no code changes for custom annotations' and 'each exporter has specific output structure'.
rabbit_holes.mitigation: Each exporter has a fixed output template structure, but annotation-to-field mappings within that template are driven by the propagation table. Custom annotations can declare which propagation targets they affect and what values to inject (e.g., arbiter_tier_override, sentinel_severity, pact_contract_assertion). The exporter looks up the propagation table for each field annotation and merges the results into its fixed template. New propagation targets (beyond the four built-in) are out of scope.

rabbit_holes.description: Foreign key blast radius in migration analysis: the spec says 'Foreign key annotations include referenced table in blast radius.' Tracing FK references across tables means the migration planner must load and cross-reference all schemas, not just the one being migrated. If FK chains are deep (A→B→C), we could spend significant time on a graph traversal engine.
rabbit_holes.mitigation: Limit blast radius to one hop: if a migration affects table A, and table B has a foreign_key annotation referencing A, table B is included in the blast radius. Do not follow transitive FK chains (B→C is not included). The blast radius is informational — it appears in the migration plan output but does not change the gate decision. Gate logic only evaluates annotations on the directly modified columns.

rabbit_holes.description: HTTP API startup time constraint (under 3 seconds): FastAPI + uvicorn startup is normally fast, but if Ledger eagerly loads and validates all schemas at startup, a large registry could blow the 3-second budget.
rabbit_holes.mitigation: Lazy loading: the API server starts with only config parsed and routes registered. Schema store and validation are loaded on first request, not at startup. The /health endpoint responds immediately. Measure startup time in CI with a 500-schema registry to catch regressions.

rabbit_holes.description: Deterministic mock data with Faker: Faker's seeding behavior can be brittle across versions. If the number of fields or their order changes, the same seed produces different output for unchanged fields because Faker's internal RNG state has shifted.
rabbit_holes.mitigation: Create a separate Faker instance per field (not per table) seeded with hash(global_seed + backend_id + table + field_name). This isolates each field's random stream so that adding/removing other fields doesn't change existing field outputs. Document this as a contract: same seed + same field definition = same value, regardless of other fields in the table.

rabbit_holes.description: File-level locking with fcntl: fcntl locks are advisory on most systems and don't work on Windows or across NFS mounts. If anyone runs Ledger on Windows or on a shared filesystem, the locking is silently ineffective.
rabbit_holes.mitigation: Acceptable for v1: the spec says single-process uvicorn and CLI commands acquire locks before writing. Document that Ledger requires a POSIX filesystem. Windows support is explicitly out of scope for this cycle. For NFS, recommend running Ledger local to the .ledger/ directory.

rabbit_holes.description: REQUIRES satisfaction semantics are underspecified for the OR case: ('gdpr_erasable', 'soft_delete_marker', 'OR', 'erasure_method_declared') — does the OR mean the same field must have erasure_method_declared, or any field in the table? And REQUIRES violations are warnings, not errors, which means they're easy to ignore and the validation output format needs to distinguish severity levels.
rabbit_holes.mitigation: Implement as: if field has gdpr_erasable, check (a) the same table has a field with soft_delete_marker annotation, OR (b) the same field has erasure_method_declared annotation. The OR binds the two alternatives: table-level for the first, field-level for the second. Validation output uses a Severity enum (ERROR for CONFLICTS, WARNING for REQUIRES) so callers can filter. The CLI exits 0 on warnings-only, non-zero on errors.

rabbit_holes.description: Canary registration with Arbiter: the spec says canary fingerprints are registered with Arbiter if configured, but the Arbiter API contract is not defined. We could spend time designing and implementing an HTTP client for an API that doesn't exist yet or changes under us.
rabbit_holes.mitigation: Define a minimal CanaryRegistrar interface (register_canary(fingerprint, metadata) -> bool). Implement a real HTTP client that POSTs to arbiter_api from config, and a no-op stub that logs a warning. The HTTP client uses httpx with a 5-second timeout. If Arbiter returns non-2xx or is unreachable, log warning and continue. Test with httpx mock fixtures only. Do not block mock generation on Arbiter availability.

rabbit_holes.description: Export format fidelity: the spec references 'complete YAML examples' in the brief for all four export formats, but we only have high-level descriptions. If the consuming tools (Pact, Arbiter, Baton, Sentinel) expect exact field names or structures, our exports could be silently incompatible.
rabbit_holes.mitigation: Define Pydantic models for each export format based on the descriptions provided (PactAssertion, ArbiterRule, BatonEgressNode, SentinelMapping). These models ARE the contract. Export tests validate that output round-trips through the Pydantic model. If consuming tools need adjustments, the Pydantic model is the single place to update. Ship with example output files in docs/ for each format.

rabbit_holes.description: Append-only changelog growth: JSONL changelog with every schema change, backend registration, migration plan, and approval will grow unbounded. On a long-lived project this file could become very large, slowing down any operation that needs to read history.
rabbit_holes.mitigation: No operation in v1 reads the full changelog — it is write-only for audit purposes. Schema state is reconstructed from the registry files, not the changelog. Document that the changelog is for audit/compliance trail only. A future 'ledger changelog compact' command can archive old entries, but it's out of scope for this cycle.

no_gos: No microservices — Ledger is a single Python package with CLI and embedded HTTP API
No ORM and no database connections — Ledger reads and stores schema YAML, it never connects to actual storage backends
No schema YAML normalization — files are stored verbatim, never reformatted or reordered on ingestion
No MySQL, Flyway, or Liquibase parser support in this cycle — SQL (PostgreSQL/ANSI) only, Alembic second if time permits
No component lifecycle management — Ledger tracks backend ownership via --owner flag, it does not manage component registration
No configurable classification tiers — the five-tier enumeration (PUBLIC, PII, FINANCIAL, AUTH, COMPLIANCE) is fixed and hardcoded
No real encryption or tokenization — Ledger generates token-shaped mock values (tok_ prefix) but does not perform actual cryptographic operations
No multi-worker API deployment — uvicorn runs single-process; concurrent write safety uses file-level locking only
No SQL execution — the migration parser extracts structural DDL changes via regex/pattern matching, never runs queries
No sqlglot or AST-based SQL parsing — regex-based extraction is acceptable for v1
